project_summary:
Collapse Aitri Hub to a single web-only surface. The `aitri-hub` binary retains only `web`, `integration review`, `help`, and `--version`; the `init`, `setup`, and `monitor` subcommands (and their deprecation stubs in `lib/commands/monitor.js` and `lib/commands/setup.js`) are removed entirely. Project registration, folder scans, and project edits move exclusively to the existing localhost admin UI at `/admin`, which is already backed by `/api/projects` CRUD inside `lib/commands/web.js`. The React empty-state in `OverviewTab.jsx`, the README, the DEPLOYMENT guide, and the parent `IDEA.md` are rewritten to describe Hub as a single-command, browser-first, localhost-only tool — Docker is demoted from 'primary deployment' to 'optional alternative'. This feature supersedes parent FR-001 (CLI setup) and FR-005 (CLI monitor) and narrows parent FR-006 (web dashboard via Docker) to 'web dashboard via embedded Node HTTP server'.

Product Analysis:
  North Star KPI: Time-to-dashboard — on a fresh clone with no prior Hub state, a developer runs `npm install && aitri-hub web`, clicks 'Add project' in the browser, and sees their first project card rendered in ≤60 seconds total, without consulting external documentation.
  JTBD: 'When I install Hub, let me open one process and be productive in the browser — without reading docs to learn a CLI wizard that duplicates what the web UI already does.'
  Guardrail metric: Collection-cycle performance and admin API behavior must not regress — parent NFR-001 (≤5s for 20 projects) and FR-006 per-project card rendering must still pass after the CLI surface is removed.
no_go_zone: No migration of `aitri-hub integration review <version>` to the web UI — the command stays as a CLI subcommand in this feature; a web-based review flow is a separate, future feature

No removal of Docker assets (`Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`, `docker/nginx.conf`) from the repository — they remain as an optional deployment path; only the documentation is rewritten to demote them from primary to optional
No authentication, no multi-user support, and no exposure of the web dashboard beyond `127.0.0.1` — the localhost-only security posture of parent NFR-005 is preserved unchanged
No changes to the on-disk format of `~/.aitri-hub/projects.json` or `~/.aitri-hub/dashboard.json` — existing registrations must keep working without any migration step

No changes to collector logic (`lib/collector/*`), alerts engine (`lib/alerts/engine.js`), or the `/api/projects` CRUD endpoints — the admin API is the canonical path and is treated as already-correct
No rename of `docker/web-dist/` → `web/dist/` in this feature — purely cosmetic refactor, deferred
No new CLI subcommands are introduced — this feature only removes; it does not add new verbs

user_personas.goal: Get a working dashboard showing at least one project visible in the browser within one minute of installing the package, using only the commands printed by `aitri-hub help`
user_personas.pain_point: Today the user must read the README, run `aitri-hub setup` in a terminal, answer ≥5 interactive prompts, then separately run a different command to view the dashboard — the extra hop is a dropout point and contradicts the fact that the browser UI already has an 'Add project' panel

user_personas.goal: Upgrade to the new version without losing their registered projects and without re-learning the CLI surface
user_personas.pain_point: The existing user's muscle memory is `aitri-hub monitor`; if that command silently keeps working as a stub, the user never discovers the web UI. If it disappears abruptly with no guidance, the user thinks Hub is broken

user_personas.goal: Read README.md or DEPLOYMENT.md and leave with an accurate mental model of how Hub runs today
user_personas.pain_point: Today the docs describe a dual-surface CLI-or-web tool backed by Docker + nginx; this disagrees with the actual product (single Node server, embedded admin UI), leaving the reader confused about which instructions are current

functional_requirements.title: Single CLI surface — only `web`, `integration review`, `help`, `--version`
functional_requirements.description: The `aitri-hub` binary exposes exactly four recognised inputs: `web` (start the server), `integration review <version>` (existing CHANGELOG attestation, unchanged), `help` / `--help` / `-h` (usage), and `version` / `--version` (package version). No other subcommands are routed. The `USAGE` help text lists only these four. Any other argument exits with a non-zero status and a message of the form `Unknown command: '<name>'. Run 'aitri-hub help' for usage.`
acceptance_criteria: Running `aitri-hub help` prints a usage block that contains the literal tokens `web`, `integration review`, `help`, `--version` and contains none of the literal tokens `init`, `setup`, `monitor`
Running `aitri-hub web` starts the HTTP server on 127.0.0.1 and the process exits 0 on SIGINT (unchanged from today)
Running `aitri-hub integration review 0.1.80` still records the manifest with exit code 0 — behavior is preserved byte-for-byte from the current implementation
Running `aitri-hub monitor`, `aitri-hub setup`, or `aitri-hub init` each exit with a non-zero status code and stderr containing the substring `Unknown command`

functional_requirements.title: Remove `init`, `setup`, `monitor` command modules and their tests
functional_requirements.description: The files `lib/commands/init.js`, `lib/commands/setup.js`, and `lib/commands/monitor.js` are deleted. The dispatcher in `bin/aitri-hub.js` no longer imports or references `cmdInit`, `cmdSetup`, or `cmdMonitor`. The unit test `tests/unit/monitor-stub.test.js` and the integration test `tests/integration/setup.test.js` are deleted, along with any other test case whose sole purpose is to exercise the removed commands. No deprecation stubs remain in the codebase.
acceptance_criteria: `git ls-files` returns no entry for `lib/commands/init.js`, `lib/commands/setup.js`, or `lib/commands/monitor.js`
`grep -r cmdInit\|cmdSetup\|cmdMonitor lib bin tests` returns zero matches
`npm test` passes after the deletion — no test file references a removed command or deprecated stub
`bin/aitri-hub.js` imports exactly one command module per recognised subcommand (web, integration-review) — no dead imports remain

functional_requirements.title: Browser-first empty state replaces the terminal wizard
functional_requirements.description: When `aitri-hub web` is started with no entries in `~/.aitri-hub/projects.json` (or the file absent), the server still starts normally and the browser, when opened, shows an empty-state panel in `OverviewTab.jsx` whose primary CTA is a link to `/admin`. The current empty-state text that instructs users to run `aitri-hub setup` and `aitri-hub monitor` is removed. No terminal prompts block the server from starting.
acceptance_criteria: Given `~/.aitri-hub/projects.json` is absent and `aitri-hub web` is run in a detached shell (no stdin), when the server starts, then it binds on 127.0.0.1:3000 and emits the existing `✓ Dashboard running` log line within 5 seconds — the process does not block waiting for input

Given the empty-state condition, when `/` is fetched in a browser, then the rendered HTML contains a link or button whose target is `/admin` and does NOT contain the literal strings `aitri-hub setup` or `aitri-hub monitor`
Given a user clicks the empty-state CTA, when `/admin` loads, then the existing admin UI renders the 'Add project' form — no new admin UI surface is introduced by this feature
The empty-state panel is visible without horizontal scroll at a 768px viewport width (continuity with parent FR-006 AC)

functional_requirements.title: Documentation reflects web-only, Docker-optional posture
functional_requirements.description: `README.md`, `DEPLOYMENT.md`, and the parent `IDEA.md` are rewritten so that: (a) the primary Quick Start is `npm install && aitri-hub web`; (b) the Features / Business Rules / Success Criteria sections no longer describe a CLI dashboard mode or reference `aitri-hub monitor` / `aitri-hub setup`; (c) Docker is presented as an optional alternative deployment, not the primary web path; (d) the architecture diagram in README reflects the embedded Node server (no separate CLI-Monitor → Docker-nginx flow); (e) env-var tables rename `AITRI_HUB_REFRESH_MS` from 'CLI monitor refresh' to 'collector refresh interval'.
acceptance_criteria: `grep -nE 'aitri-hub (monitor|setup|init)' README.md DEPLOYMENT.md IDEA.md` returns zero matches
README.md contains a Quick Start section whose commands are exactly `npm install` and `aitri-hub web` (in that order) and no other setup step is presented as required
DEPLOYMENT.md contains a section explicitly marked 'Optional: Docker deployment' (or equivalent wording containing the token 'optional') wrapping all Docker / nginx instructions
The parent `IDEA.md` Business Rules section no longer contains the phrase 'CLI terminal dashboard' and the Success Criteria no longer reference `aitri-hub monitor`
The architecture diagram in README.md (ASCII or prose) shows a single Node.js process that both serves the UI and runs the collector — not two separate components

functional_requirements.title: Zero-migration upgrade path for existing users
functional_requirements.description: A user who previously registered projects via the removed `aitri-hub setup` command (producing a `~/.aitri-hub/projects.json` with schema version 1) must be able to upgrade to this version and run `aitri-hub web` with no extra step — their existing project entries must continue to appear in the dashboard. The `projects.json` read path in `lib/store/projects.js` is not altered by this feature; the `defaultInterface` field written by the old `init.js` wizard is ignored if present and is not required.
acceptance_criteria: Given a `~/.aitri-hub/projects.json` file produced by the current `init.js` wizard (containing `version`, `defaultInterface`, and `projects[]`), when `aitri-hub web` is run post-upgrade, then all entries in `projects[]` appear in the dashboard — count matches and each entry's `name` and `location` render unchanged
Given the upgraded `aitri-hub web` is running, when the user adds a project via `/admin`, then the written `projects.json` remains valid JSON and round-trips through a subsequent server restart
The `projects.json` schema on disk after an upgrade+add cycle contains at minimum the fields `{ projects: [{ id, name, type, location, addedAt }] }` — no required field is dropped
No CHANGELOG entry or release note instructs the user to run a migration script — the upgrade is purely a `npm install` replacement

functional_requirements.title: Admin UI is the only registration surface — and it is already complete
functional_requirements.description: All project registration, edit, scan, and removal flows happen exclusively through the existing `/admin` route, which is already backed by `GET|POST|PUT|DELETE /api/projects` in `lib/commands/web.js`. This feature does not add or modify any admin API endpoint; it asserts that the existing endpoints cover every use case previously served by `aitri-hub setup` and `aitri-hub setup --scan`. If a gap is found during implementation, it is recorded as technical debt and handled in a separate feature — it does NOT justify keeping the CLI surface alive.
acceptance_criteria: The existing `/api/projects` endpoints support POST (create), PUT (edit), DELETE (remove), and a scan path — verified by running the current test suite against them with no code changes from this feature
The `/admin` UI surfaces all four operations (add, edit, delete, scan folder) with no feature work required in `hub-web-only`
A gap discovered during implementation is filed as a new feature or backlog item — it does NOT re-introduce `lib/commands/setup.js` or any other CLI command
The `/api/projects` localhost-only guard (127.0.0.1 / ::1 check) is preserved — non-localhost requests still receive 403

user_stories.i_want: to run `aitri-hub help` and see exactly the commands that exist today
user_stories.so_that: I do not waste time trying commands the README once documented but that no longer work

acceptance_criteria.given: a fresh install of aitri-hub at the post-feature version
acceptance_criteria.then: stdout contains a line mentioning `web` and a line mentioning `integration review`, and contains no line mentioning `init`, `setup`, or `monitor`
the process exits with status code ≠ 0 and stderr contains the substring `Unknown command`

user_stories.i_want: the `init`, `setup`, and `monitor` modules fully deleted (not just stubs)
user_stories.so_that: dead code does not rot in the repo and confuse future contributors reading `lib/commands/`

acceptance_criteria.when: `git ls-files lib/commands/ tests/unit/ tests/integration/` is run
acceptance_criteria.then: the output contains no path matching `init.js`, `setup.js`, `monitor.js`, `monitor-stub.test.js`, or `setup.test.js`
the test suite exits with code 0 and no skipped/pending test references the removed commands

user_stories.as_a: first-time Hub user with an empty registry
user_stories.i_want: the browser itself to show me how to add my first project
user_stories.so_that: I do not have to leave the browser and guess a CLI incantation to get started

acceptance_criteria.given: no `~/.aitri-hub/projects.json` exists and `aitri-hub web` has just started
acceptance_criteria.then: the page renders a non-error empty state whose primary CTA navigates to `/admin`

acceptance_criteria.when: the rendered HTML body is inspected
acceptance_criteria.then: it contains no occurrence of the strings `aitri-hub setup` or `aitri-hub monitor`

user_stories.i_want: the README, DEPLOYMENT, and IDEA documents to describe the product that actually runs
user_stories.so_that: I can trust the docs as a reference instead of treating them as historical fiction

acceptance_criteria.when: its Quick Start section is read
acceptance_criteria.then: the only commands presented as required are `npm install` and `aitri-hub web`
they appear under a section whose heading contains the token `optional` (case-insensitive)

user_stories.i_want: my existing `~/.aitri-hub/projects.json` to keep working without any migration step
user_stories.so_that: the upgrade is a one-command replacement and my registered projects survive

acceptance_criteria.given: a `~/.aitri-hub/projects.json` written by the old `init.js` wizard containing 3 project entries
acceptance_criteria.then: all 3 projects appear in the dashboard with their original `name` and `location`

acceptance_criteria.given: the upgraded server is running against the legacy-format projects.json
acceptance_criteria.when: the user adds a 4th project via `/admin`
acceptance_criteria.then: the file is rewritten as valid JSON, survives a server restart, and all 4 projects render

user_stories.i_want: to confirm that the admin UI and `/api/projects` already cover every CLI flow being removed
user_stories.so_that: deleting the CLI commands does not lose functionality

acceptance_criteria.then: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE on `/api/projects` are all present and the folder-scan flow has a corresponding frontend surface — verified by the existing test suite passing with no change
the server still responds 403 (the localhost guard from FR-016 is preserved)

non_functional_requirements.requirement: A developer with no prior Hub knowledge reaches a working dashboard with at least one project visible in ≤60 seconds from running `npm install`, using only what `aitri-hub help` and the browser UI tell them
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Timed run on a fresh clone: `npm install && aitri-hub web` → browser opens → user clicks 'Add project' in `/admin` and enters a valid local path → dashboard shows the project card. Total elapsed wall-clock time ≤60s on a machine meeting parent NFR-004

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Running `aitri-hub web` in a non-interactive shell (e.g. nohup, systemd, CI runner) must not block waiting for stdin at any point — removal of the wizard must not introduce any hidden prompt
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: `aitri-hub web </dev/null >/tmp/web.log 2>&1 &` on a machine with no existing `~/.aitri-hub/projects.json` prints the `Dashboard running` log line within 5 seconds and the process remains alive until SIGINT

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Collection-cycle time must not regress — parent NFR-001 (≤5s for 20 projects) continues to hold after the CLI surface is removed
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Benchmark against the same 20-project fixture used by the existing collector integration test: post-feature mean cycle time is within ±10% of the pre-feature mean

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The HTTP server continues to log every request in the existing format `[ISO8601] METHOD /path STATUS` — this feature removes surfaces but does not reduce logging
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: After running the post-feature `aitri-hub web` and hitting `/api/projects` with GET and POST, stdout contains at least two log lines matching the regex `^\[[0-9T:.Z-]+\] (GET|POST) /api/projects [0-9]{3}$`

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Localhost-only admin API guard is preserved — non-127.0.0.1 / non-::1 requests to `/api/projects` still receive 403
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: A request to `/api/projects` from a simulated non-localhost peer (e.g. via a test that patches `req.socket.remoteAddress`) receives HTTP 403 with body `{"error":"forbidden"}`

constraints: Zero npm runtime dependencies — the CLI shrinks, but the zero-deps invariant of the parent project is preserved
Node.js ≥18.0.0 — no change to the runtime requirement
All persistent data continues to live under `~/.aitri-hub/` — no new directories, no new config files
No breaking changes to `/api/projects` contracts — web/ frontend is source-compatible without edits beyond the empty-state text
technology_preferences: No new dependencies introduced by this feature
Deletions preferred over deprecation stubs — the repository is small enough that a clean removal is safer than a long stub tail
Documentation rewrites are in-place edits of the existing files, not parallel new files
